original_hypothesis.title: Router._validateDecimals Underflows When baseDecimals > quoteDecimals
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Router._validateDecimals performs `quoteDecimals - baseDecimals` as uint8 subtraction. In Solidity 0.8+, this underflows and reverts when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals. This prevents creating any pool via Router where the base token has more decimals than the quote token, even if the absolute difference is within acceptable range (e.g., base=18, quote=8).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The validation is asymmetric - it only works when quoteDecimals >= baseDecimals. The developer likely intended to check absolute difference but performed one-directional subtraction.

proof_of_signal.pattern: quoteDecimals - baseDecimals underflows as uint8 when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals

preconditions: User attempts to create a pool where base token decimals > quote token decimals

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: if (quoteDecimals - baseDecimals > MAX_BASE_QUOTE_DECIMALS_DIFFERENCE) - uint8 subtraction underflows
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Asymmetric validation blocks legitimate pool creation for certain decimal combinations
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No handling for baseDecimals > quoteDecimals case

analysis_questions: What token pairs are intended to be created via Router?
Can pools be created directly via Factory, bypassing Router validation?
Are there deployed pools with baseDecimals > quoteDecimals?
validation_requirements: Attempt createPool with base=WBTC(8 decimals) and quote=USDC(6 decimals) - should work but reverts
Verify that Factory.create works directly without this limitation

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not an attack but a code defect. Any user trying to create a pool where base has more decimals than quote will have the transaction revert. They would need to swap the token order or use Factory.create directly.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: DoS for pool creation with certain token pairs via Router
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Users can use Factory.create directly as a workaround

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for createPool with base decimals > quote decimals

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediately observable when attempting pool creation

validation_result.impact_summary: Router._validateDecimals reverts on underflow when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals, blocking pool creation via Router for legitimate token pairs (e.g., WBTC/USDC, or createPoolETH with useTokenAsQuote=true for most tokens).
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The `_validateDecimals` function in Router.sol performs `quoteDecimals - baseDecimals` as a uint8 subtraction. Under Solidity 0.8.20 (confirmed in foundry.toml: `solc_version = "0.8.20"`), unsigned integer underflow causes an automatic revert. When `baseDecimals > quoteDecimals`, this subtraction underflows and reverts, preventing pool creation through the Router for any token pair where the base token has more decimals than the quote token.

This affects two entry points:
1. `createPool`: Directly calls `_validateDecimals(baseToken.decimals(), quoteToken.decimals())`. If base has 18 decimals and quote has 8, the call reverts.
2. `createPoolETH`: When `useTokenAsQuote=true`, calls `_validateDecimals(18, token.decimals())`. Since WETH has 18 decimals and most tokens have fewer (e.g., USDC=6, USDT=6), this path reverts for nearly all tokens.

The developer clearly intended to check the absolute difference between decimals (the error message is `ErrDecimalsDifferenceTooLarge` and the constant is `MAX_BASE_QUOTE_DECIMALS_DIFFERENCE`), but implemented only a one-directional check. The Factory.create function has no such validation, confirming this is a Router-specific defect that can be bypassed by calling Factory directly.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is accurate. Router.sol's `_validateDecimals` function contains the exact vulnerable pattern described: `quoteDecimals - baseDecimals` as uint8 subtraction that underflows when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals. The Solidity version is 0.8.20, which has checked arithmetic by default.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When baseDecimals > quoteDecimals (e.g., base=18, quote=8), the expression `quoteDecimals - baseDecimals` underflows as a uint8 subtraction in Solidity 0.8+, causing an automatic revert. The check should use the absolute difference.
Calls _validateDecimals with baseToken.decimals() as first arg. If base has more decimals than quote, the function reverts due to uint8 underflow in _validateDecimals.
When useTokenAsQuote=true, base=WETH (18 decimals) and quote=token. For any token with decimals < 18 (most tokens), this underflows. E.g., USDC (6 decimals): 6 - 18 underflows.

calling_sequence: 6. _validateDecimals evaluates: quoteDecimals - baseDecimals = 6 - 8 → uint8 underflow → REVERT
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path from createPool/createPoolETH to _validateDecimals is direct and unconditional. The underflow occurs whenever baseDecimals > quoteDecimals, regardless of the actual difference magnitude. For createPoolETH with useTokenAsQuote=true, this affects virtually all tokens since WETH has 18 decimals and most tokens have fewer.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: foundry.toml confirms solc_version = "0.8.20" which has checked arithmetic. MIMSwap.s.sol deploys BlastMIMSwapRouter. No special configuration affects this pure arithmetic bug.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack but a code defect causing DoS. Any user attempting to create a pool via Router where baseDecimals > quoteDecimals (e.g., WBTC(8)/USDC(6), or createPoolETH with useTokenAsQuote=true for USDC, USDT, etc.) will have the transaction revert. The impact is significant for createPoolETH with useTokenAsQuote=true since WETH has 18 decimals and nearly all tokens have fewer decimals, effectively blocking that code path entirely.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. The Router is the primary user-facing contract for pool creation. The defect blocks createPool for token pairs where base has more decimals than quote, and blocks createPoolETH with useTokenAsQuote=true for virtually all tokens. Users can work around this by calling Factory.create directly or swapping the base/quote order, but this degrades UX and may confuse integrators.
recommended_mitigations: Replace the subtraction with an absolute difference check: `uint8 diff = baseDecimals > quoteDecimals ? baseDecimals - quoteDecimals : quoteDecimals - baseDecimals; if (diff > MAX_BASE_QUOTE_DECIMALS_DIFFERENCE) { revert ErrDecimalsDifferenceTooLarge(); }`

Alternatively, cast to larger type before subtraction: `if (uint256(quoteDecimals) + MAX_BASE_QUOTE_DECIMALS_DIFFERENCE < uint256(baseDecimals) || uint256(baseDecimals) + MAX_BASE_QUOTE_DECIMALS_DIFFERENCE < uint256(quoteDecimals)) { revert ErrDecimalsDifferenceTooLarge(); }`
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Router with valid weth and factory addresses. 2. Call createPool with two tokens where baseToken.decimals() > quoteToken.decimals() (e.g., mock tokens with 18 and 6 decimals). 3. Observe revert due to arithmetic underflow. 4. Also test createPoolETH with useTokenAsQuote=true and a token with < 18 decimals.
additional_testing_needed: Verify which real token pairs in the Blast ecosystem would be affected (WBTC/USDC, etc.)
Test createPoolETH with useTokenAsQuote=true to confirm it reverts for common tokens
